message:
copy: final prose tightening — cut throat-clearing, shorter headings, direct voice

A few remaining copyediting refinements that tighten the most verbose
introductory sentences across the article:

Packets Are Equations:
- "To understand why this isn't magic, we have to stop looking at packets as
  'chunks of a file' and start looking at them as Linear Equations" → "The key
  reframing: stop thinking of packets as 'chunks of a file' and start thinking
  of them as linear equations" (shorter, imperative, lowercase "linear equations")
- "Let's make it concrete. Suppose your file..." → "Suppose your file..." (cut
  the meta-commentary; the example IS the concreteness)

Coupon Collector's Tax:
- "a new villain: the Coupon Collector Problem" → "the Coupon Collector Problem"
  (drop redundant "a new villain:" framing)
- "The Probability You Should Tattoo On Your Intuition" → "The Key Probability"
  (shorter box heading, less trying-too-hard)
- "The whole story of fountain code design is threading this needle" → "Fountain
  code design is about navigating this tradeoff" (more direct)

Comparisons:
- "The best way to appreciate RaptorQ is to see what it refuses to be." → "How
  does RaptorQ compare to the other options?" (a question is more inviting than
  a proclamation)

Where RaptorQ Lives:
- "RaptorQ's properties (fungible symbols, near-zero overhead, linear-time
  encoding/decoding, ratelessness) don't just make for elegant theory. They
  fundamentally change..." → "Fungible symbols, near-zero overhead, linear-time
  encoding/decoding, ratelessness. These properties change..." (list-then-assert
  is punchier than parenthetical-then-disclaim)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -221,10 +221,10 @@

Packets Are Equations

- To understand why this isn't magic, we have to stop looking at packets as "chunks of a file" and start looking at them as Linear Equations. + The key reframing: stop thinking of packets as "chunks of a file" and start thinking of them as linear equations.

- Let's make it concrete. Suppose your file is four bytes: A=5, B=3, C=7, D=2. Instead of sending those four values directly, you generate encoded packets by XORing subsets: + Suppose your file is four bytes: A=5, B=3, C=7, D=2. Instead of sending those four values directly, you generate encoded packets by XORing subsets:

Packet 1: A ⊕ C = 5 ⊕ 7 = 2   [1,0,1,0]
@@ -331,7 +331,7 @@

The Coupon Collector's Tax

Dense: solving a system of \(K\) random linear equations takes \(O(K^3)\) time (Gaussian Elimination). If your file has 50,000 blocks, \(50,000^3\) is 125 trillion operations. Your CPU would melt before the video started playing.

- So we make the equations sparse: instead of XORing 50% of the symbols, we XOR only a few (say, 5 or 10). This makes solving fast. But sparsity introduces a new villain: the Coupon Collector Problem. + So we make the equations sparse: instead of XORing 50% of the symbols, we XOR only a few (say, 5 or 10). This makes solving fast. But sparsity introduces the Coupon Collector Problem.

A Subtle Failure Mode
@@ -351,7 +351,7 @@

The Coupon Collector's Tax

-
The Probability You Should Tattoo On Your Intuition
+
The Key Probability

Suppose each encoded packet touches exactly \(d\) randomly chosen source symbols. After receiving \(n\) packets, you've made about \(d \cdot n\) random "touches" across \(K\) symbols. Pick one specific symbol \(x_i\). The probability it was never touched is:

@@ -372,7 +372,7 @@

The Coupon Collector's Tax

We want something that behaves like \(K + O(1)\), not \(K \log K\).

- The whole story of fountain code design is threading this needle. Dense equations give you solvability but \(O(K^3)\) decoding. Sparse equations give you speed but the coupon collector's log tax on overhead. It seemed like a fundamental tradeoff. + Fountain code design is about navigating this tradeoff. Dense equations give you solvability but \(O(K^3)\) decoding. Sparse equations give you speed but the coupon collector's log tax on overhead.

@@ -984,7 +984,7 @@

Same Continent, Different Co

RaptorQ vs. The Alternatives

- The best way to appreciate RaptorQ is to see what it refuses to be. + How does RaptorQ compare to the other options?

@@ -1112,7 +1112,7 @@

Spectral Theory (Where It Actually

Where RaptorQ Lives

- RaptorQ's properties (fungible symbols, near-zero overhead, linear-time encoding/decoding, ratelessness) don't just make for elegant theory. They fundamentally change the economics of data distribution in the real world. + Fungible symbols, near-zero overhead, linear-time encoding/decoding, ratelessness. These properties change the economics of data distribution.

@@ -1194,9 +1194,9 @@

ATSC 3.0 (NextGen TV)

-

Beyond Networking: Fountain Codes as a Systems Primitive

+

Fountain Codes as a Systems Primitive

- The deployments above use RaptorQ the way its inventors intended: as a network transport layer. But what happens when you treat fountain codes not as a wire protocol but as a foundational abstraction for building systems? Three projects by the author of this article explore that question. + The deployments above use RaptorQ as a network transport layer. But fountain codes can also serve as a building block for systems that have nothing to do with networking. Three projects by the author of this article explore that idea.

@@ -1279,7 +1279,7 @@

A Symbol-First Mesh Protocol for A

- The pattern across all three projects: RaptorQ stops being a "network layer" and becomes an information substrate. In FrankenSQLite, every page on disk is fountain-coded. In asupersync, every runtime state checkpoint is fountain-coded. In Flywheel Connectors, every object in transit is fountain-coded. The "any \(K\) of \(N\)" property extends beyond surviving packet loss. It enables systems where data integrity is a mathematical invariant rather than a best-effort aspiration. + The common thread: In all three projects, RaptorQ moves from the network layer into the storage and runtime layers. FrankenSQLite fountain-codes every page on disk. Asupersync fountain-codes every runtime checkpoint. Flywheel Connectors fountain-codes every object in transit. The "any \(K\) of \(N\)" property makes data integrity a mathematical guarantee rather than a best-effort goal.
